incident.incident_name: Promotions rules-engine evaluation exhausts CPU on shared node
incident.description: Resource Exhaustion incident on the Finance pipeline (RTL_FIN_RES): promotions rules-engine evaluation exhausts CPU on shared node.
incident.symptoms: The real-time-adjacent promotions rules-evaluation task, which re-evaluates all active promotion rules against the full active-cart dataset every 15 minutes, regularly saturates its node's CPU during high-traffic promotional events.
incident.root_cause: The rules engine evaluates all rules against all carts in a nested-loop pattern (O(rules x carts)) rather than an indexed/rule-triggered evaluation approach, and a major promotional event tripled both active cart count and active rule count simultaneously.
incident.business_impact: Promotion evaluation lag during a live flash-sale event caused some qualifying carts to not receive their discount before checkout completed, generating post-hoc customer service escalations and manual discount honoring.
incident.resolution: Refactor the rules engine to an indexed/triggered evaluation model (evaluate only rules relevant to a cart's contents) instead of full nested-loop evaluation; add horizontal scaling for this task during declared promotional events.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: The rules engine evaluates all rules against all carts in a nested-loop pattern (O(rules x carts)) rather than an indexed/rule-triggered evaluation approach, and a major promotional event tripled both active cart count and active rule count simultaneously.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow task duration metric shows non-linear (multiplicative) growth matching the CPU saturation pattern
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog container.cpu.usage for the rules-evaluation task shows a clear correlation with the product of cart_count x active_rule_count, not either alone
expected_rca.resolution: Refactor the rules engine to an indexed/triggered evaluation model (evaluate only rules relevant to a cart's contents) instead of full nested-loop evaluation; add horizontal scaling for this task during declared promotional events.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Add algorithmic-complexity review as a required design-gate in the architecture template for any task whose complexity scales multiplicatively with two independently-growing inputs.
